title: System and method of mobile based user authentication for an access controlled environment
abstract: A method of mobile based user authentication includes storing a plurality of categories of data associated with user activity with a mobile device carried or worn by a user. The method also includes analyzing the plurality of categories of data over a recent period of time to determine a user trust score based on a historical period of time, the historical period of time greater than the recent period of time. The method further includes granting access to an access controlled environment if the user trust score is within a predetermined score range.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method of mobile based user authentication comprising: storing a plurality of categories of data associated with user activity with a mobile device carried or worn by a user; analyzing the plurality of categories of data over a recent period of time to determine a user trust score based on a historical period of time, the historical period of time greater than the recent period of time; and granting access to an access controlled environment if the user trust score is within a predetermined score range. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein analyzing the plurality of categories of data is initiated once the mobile device is located within a predetermined distance of the access controlled environment. 3. The method of any of the preceding claims, further comprising granting access to the access controlled environment subsequent to the user providing a user intent signal. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the user intent signal comprises tapping the mobile device. 5. The method of claim 3, wherein the user intent signal comprises shaking the mobile device. 6. The method of claim 3, wherein the user intent signal comprises providing a voice command. 7. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein granting access to the access controlled environment comprises unlocking a lock associated with an entry point of the access controlled environment. 8. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the plurality of categories of data comprises at least one of location history, mobile device use, activity level, and biometrics. 9. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the access controlled environment is a residential home. 10. The method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the access controlled environment is a commercial office space. 11. A method of mobile based user authentication comprising: storing a plurality of categories of data associated with user activity with a mobile device carried or worn by a user to determine a user pattern over a historical period of time; 

 analyzing the plurality of categories of data over a recent period of time to determine a user trust score based on the user pattern, the recent period of time less than the historical period of time; analyzing a risk value as a function of a plurality of variables, the user trust score one of the plurality of variables; and granting access to an access controlled environment if the risk value is within a predetermined value. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein analyzing the plurality of categories of data is initiated once the mobile device is located within a predetermined distance of the access controlled environment. 13. The method of claim 11 or 12, further comprising granting access to the access controlled environment subsequent to the user providing a user intent signal. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the user intent signal comprises tapping the mobile device. 15. The method of claim 13, wherein the user intent signal comprises shaking the mobile device. 16. The method of claim 13, wherein the user intent signal comprises providing a voice command. 17. The method of any of claims 11-16, wherein granting access to the access controlled environment comprises unlocking a lock associated with an entry point of the access controlled environment. 18. The method of any of claims 11-17, wherein the plurality of categories of data comprises at least one of location history, mobile device use, activity level, and biometrics. 19. The method of any of claims 11-18, wherein the access controlled environment is a residential home. 20. The method of any of claims 11-18, wherein the access controlled environment is a commercial office space.
